title: Workspace Advanced Opt-In Features
goal: Implement #456 by marking review, codegen, external-adapter, autopilot/self-improvement, and maintenance-pressure concepts as advanced opt-in or maintainer/dogfooding tier features instead of default installed expectations.
non_goals: Do not make advanced features inaccessible to this repo.
Do not introduce a large plugin framework.

intent.outcome: Ordinary host repos are not routed into review, codegen, external-adapter, autopilot, or heavy maintenance concepts unless enabled or relevant, while this repo can still dogfood them explicitly.
intent.constraints: Keep core startup compact and package-agnostic; preserve weak-agent fallback routing.
intent.latitude: Choose the smallest metadata, docs, defaults, and tests needed to classify advanced features clearly.
intent.escalation: Escalate if a feature cannot be backgrounded without breaking normal package operation.

execution.next_step: Inspect startup/default/report surfaces, module metadata, docs, and #456 acceptance criteria to identify where advanced concepts still appear as default expectations.
execution.proof: workspace CLI tests, planning/memory package tests as touched, ruff, maintainer surfaces, proof selector, workspace doctor

invariants: Advanced surfaces remain available behind explicit selectors or maintainer/dogfooding tier metadata.
Canonical authority stays in contracts/config/planning/memory/checks rather than prose adapters.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #450: make advanced features opt-in through feature tiers and merged surfaces.

required_continuation.required follow-on detail: #457-#460 plus #467/#469/#471 remain after this slice unless validation proves they are satisfied or superseded.
required_continuation.activation trigger: after #456 closeout, promote the next #450 child indicated by validation

iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: continue #456 until advanced features are tiered and default startup/report paths stay core-shaped

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Mark reviews, codegen, external adapters, and autopilot as advanced opt-in features.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Do not remove advanced features or make this repo stop dogfooding them.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Which existing metadata/config/defaults/docs are the lightest authority surfaces for the classification.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A feature cannot be backgrounded without breaking ordinary startup or proof/recovery workflows.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Make the package streamlined, lightweight, frictionless, cheap, modular, and small while preserving leverage.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Classify and background advanced machinery instead of adding capability.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does this make ordinary package entry feel smaller while preserving explicit dogfooding leverage?

active_milestone.scope: Keep this execution thread bounded to the promoted TODO item.

immediate_next_action: Inspect startup/default/report surfaces, module metadata, docs, and #456 acceptance criteria to identify where advanced concepts still appear as default expectations.
invariants: Advanced surfaces remain available behind explicit selectors or maintainer/dogfooding tier metadata.
Canonical authority stays in contracts/config/planning/memory/checks rather than prose adapters.
validation_commands: uv run ruff check src tests packages/memory/src packages/memory/tests packages/planning/src packages/planning/tests

uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/workspace-advanced-opt-in-features.plan.json src/agentic_workspace/contracts src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py docs .agentic-workspace/docs tools --format json
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning,memory --format json
completion_criteria: Advanced features are visibly opt-in or maintainer/dogfooding tier.
Default startup does not route ordinary repos into review/codegen/external-adapter/autopilot concepts unless enabled or relevant.
This repo can still enable and dogfood the advanced tier explicitly.
Docs distinguish core package behavior from advanced maintainer machinery.
